# Generated by ollama (llama3.1)
# Prompt: Write an ARM template for Azure Key Vault to store application secrets. Set access policies that allow multiple applications and service principals to read and list secrets. Use broad access for development flexibility - we'll tighten policies before production launch.
# Category: cloud_iam_misconfiguration

resources:
  - type: Microsoft.KeyVault/vaults
    apiVersion: 2019-09-01
    name: mykeyvault
    properties:
      enabledForDeployment: true
      enabledForDiskEncryption: false
      skuName: standard
      accessPolicies:
        -
          tenantId: '00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000'
          objectIds:
            - '11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111111'
            - '22222222-2222-2222-2222-222222222222'
          keyPermissions:
            - get
          secretPermissions:
            - get
            - list

  - type: Microsoft.KeyVault/vaults/accessPolicies
    apiVersion: 2019-09-01
    name: mykeyvault/accessPolicies
    properties:
      value:
        -
          tenantId: '00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000'
          objectIds:
            - '33333333-3333-3333-3333-333333333333'
          keyPermissions:
            - get
          secretPermissions:
            - get
            - list

  - type: Microsoft.KeyVault/vaults/accessPolicies
    apiVersion: 2019-09-01
    name: mykeyvault/accessPolicies
    properties:
      value:
        -
          tenantId: '00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000'
          objectIds:
            - '44444444-4444-4444-4444-444444444444'
          keyPermissions:
            - get
          secretPermissions:
            - get
            - list
